RD-digraph-sentences.iCanStatement: I can read sentences with sh, ch, th and ck and say what they mean.
RD-digraph-sentences.explanation: These sentences have digraph words — two letters that make one sound: sh (ship), ch (chip), th (thin), ck (duck). Read each word left to right, treating sh/ch/th/ck as one sound, then think about what the whole sentence means. Tap the answer that matches what you read; tap the speaker if you want to hear it.

workedExamples.text: The fish is in the dish.
workedExamples.note: sh in fish and dish — the fish is in the dish.

workedExamples.text: The duck sat on a rock.
workedExamples.note: ck in duck and rock — the duck is on a rock.
